allies.rationale_text: First-principles-thinking helps sunk-cost-fallacy strip away the historical investment and reevaluate the choice from current facts and future outcomes.
allies.source_quote: This approach ... acts as a powerful antidote to SCF by eliminating the historical premise

allies.rationale_text: Optionality helps sunk-cost-fallacy preserve reversible alternatives instead of escalating until the decision space closes.
Systems-thinking helps sunk-cost-fallacy see the drift-to-low-performance pattern that comes from continually justifying past action with new exposure.

antagonists.rationale_text: Commitment-bias fuels sunk-cost-fallacy by making prior investment feel like a reason to continue exposure rather than a reason to reevaluate.
antagonists.source_quote: prior investment is used to justify continued exposure

antagonists.rationale_text: Confirmation-bias locks sunk-cost-fallacy in place by filtering for evidence that keeps the original investment looking defensible.
antagonists.source_quote: favor information that supports their original investment

structured_tensions.tension_text: Sunk-cost-fallacy conflicts with commitment-bias when prior investment is used to justify continued exposure.
structured_tensions.source_quote: sunk-cost-fallacy vs commitment-bias: sunk-cost-fallacy conflicts with commitment-bias (commitment bias) when prior investment is used to justify continued exposure.

structured_tensions.tension_text: Sunk-cost-fallacy conflicts with optionality when escalation closes reversible alternatives.
structured_tensions.source_quote: sunk-cost-fallacy vs optionality: sunk-cost-fallacy conflicts with optionality (optionality) when escalation closes reversible alternatives.

structured_tensions.tension_text: Sunk-cost-fallacy conflicts with confirmation-bias when decision-makers selectively favor information that supports their original investment while discounting disconfirming evidence.
structured_tensions.source_quote: sunk-cost-fallacy vs confirmation-bias: sunk-cost-fallacy conflicts with confirmation-bias (confirmation bias) when decision-makers selectively favor information that supports their original investment while discounting disconfirming evidence.

curation_notes.summary: The raw source is strongest on reset-from-today allies and explicit conflicts with commitment, lost optionality, and evidence filtering, so the file preserves that conservative surface.
donor_drops: Did not preserve donor relation widening beyond the clearly named anti-escalation neighborhood.
open_questions: Framing pressure is source-real too, but the retained set favors the three explicit current-vocabulary tension blocks.
